conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have children. I'm asking about my one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So you have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

Now I need to ask you:

**Does Alex normally live with you, or are you responsible for paying towards their upbringing?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's helpful.

Next question:

**Are you currently living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you.

Now I need to ask:

**Are you or your partner (if you have one) subject to immigration control, or do you have any restrictions on your right to reside in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have any immigration restrictions or limitations on my right to live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

One more question:

**Is there anyone else who might also be claiming Child Benefit for Alex, such as another parent or guardian?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, there's nobody else claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the information you've provided:

✓ Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
✓ Alex lives with you and you're responsible for them
✓ You live in the UK with no immigration restrictions
✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You meet all the key eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. You can proceed with making a claim for Alex.

If you'd like information on how to apply, you can apply online or by post using the Child Benefit claim form (CH2).

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 11 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child in hospital for 11 weeks (within 12-week limit); Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 11 weeks (within 12-week limit)
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16 and therefore qualifies as a qualifying young person for Child Benefit purposes. The claimant lives with Alex in the UK and is responsible for them. The claimant has no immigration restrictions or limitations on their right to reside in the UK. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to the eligibility criteria, the claimant can get Child Benefit if they're responsible for a child under 16 (or under 20 if they stay in approved education or training) and they live in the UK. All these conditions are met for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, aged 8 years old, who lives with them in the UK. The claimant has no immigration restrictions and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All the key eligibility criteria are satisfied: the child is under 16, lives with the claimant who is responsible for them, the claimant lives in the UK with no immigration restrictions, and there are no competing claims. Therefore, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
